steps.title: Identify the key warning
steps.content: The passage cautions that small sample sizes can "contribute to reports of exaggerated effects." Pimentel's study used a sample of only 12 fish.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The question asks what the text "most directly suggests" about Pimentel's conclusion. The text links small samples to biased, exaggerated findings — apply that logic to each option.
  • Choice A — "same mean sample size used in many earlier studies": the passage never compares Pimentel's sample to earlier studies, so this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — "broad implications for other fish species": the passage says nothing about other species — an unsupported inference, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "may overstate the effect": directly mirrors the passage's warning that small samples "contribute to reports of exaggerated effects," so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "largely invalidated by similar studies": the passage flags potential bias, not full invalidation — this overclaims, so fails. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
